human_verification.notes: Reviewed affordable housing-specific compliance boundaries, HUD/HOME/IRS authority sources, LIHTC and HOME evidence expectations, tenant-file and EIV/TRACS handling, fair housing and property standards escalation, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, tax-credit, agency, fair housing, financial, investor, lender, and safety signoff.

review_notes: All required issue scenario families and cross-industry research contract families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared specialist behavior, compliance-controls-and-governance, and cross-industry artifact awareness are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to affordable housing compliance obligation mapping, evidence handling, file and unit readiness review, finding classification, remediation tracking, review preparation, and escalation support.

Authority sources cover HUD subsidized multifamily occupancy, HOME rental housing and recordkeeping, IRS/Treasury LIHTC monitoring and Form 8823 issue handling, HUD TRACS, HUD income and HOME rent limits, and HUD fair housing/Section 504 boundary guidance.

Scenario set covers incomplete tenant-file evidence, rent and utility allowance prerequisites, HOME fixed/floating conflicts, LIHTC monitoring, EIV/TRACS discrepancies, fair housing routing, investor financial boundaries, property standards escalation, low-confidence missing program stack, policy/operational conflict, tenant adaptation, and specification-based deployment gating.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated spec pack. Jurisdiction, state HFA, program stack, regulatory agreement, HOME written agreement, HAP contract, investor, lender, local covenant, tenant policy, and record-system differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated specification-based use under documented deployment and rollback controls.
